**Contributors** 

* PMs: [Moritz Natalini](mailto:m.natalini@efg.gg)[Richard Lancaster](mailto:r.lancaster@efg.gg)  
* FE: [Ilya Rabchynski](mailto:i.rabchynski@efg.gg)  
* Moba Ops/Admins: [max.ichetovkin@mobalyticshq.com](mailto:max.ichetovkin@mobalyticshq.com)[lars@mobalyticshq.com](mailto:lars@mobalyticshq.com)  
* EFG Commercial team: [Mandar Kambli](mailto:m.kambli@efg.gg)

## **Background**

Publift is currently our main partner for Rembrandt inventory (internally labelled as *indirect*), operating via a prebid solution. We originally partnered with Publift because they maintain existing relationships with the major SSPs and DSPs, which is essential for a competitive prebid auction. Building these integrations ourselves would require significant legal scrutiny, time, and operational effort. Publift charges an estimated 20% commission on total revenue.

With the Mobalytics acquisition, we now have an opportunity to leverage their **in-house prebid stack**, which the EFG Commercial team has confirmed represents a meaningful strategic opportunity.

This leads to the core question:

## **Problem Statement**

**Which prebid solution should Faceit adopt going forward: Publift or Mobalytics?**

## **Comparative Analysis**

### **Pros**

#### **Mobalytics**

* Direct relationships with SSPs and DSPs  
* Full transparency on payments, reporting, and fees  
* Zero commissions  
* Internal ownership and long-term strategic value

**Publift**

* Handles SSP & DSP onboarding end-to-end  
* Fully managed optimisation service  
* Mature operational workflows

### 

### **Cons**

#### **Mobalytics**

* Potential delays when onboarding new SSPs/DSPs due to EFG legal processes  
  * *Open question: is this actually a blocker if the most important partners are already onboarded?*

#### **Publift**

* No transparency on underlying SSP/DSP fees  
* 20% revenue commission  
* Less control over the auction mechanics

## **Proposed Approach**

**Run an A/B test comparing Mobalytics Prebid vs. Publift Prebid.**

We identified **CSStats** as the ideal environment because it has sufficient traffic to reach statistical significance while minimising revenue risk to the core platform.

### **Traffic Snapshot ([CSStats](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1DFZK2jUFlH6Qk7ZqU7KfrOdPDjHEFbTm67ToFBjYKc0/edit?gid=1861885688#gid=1861885688))**

* **Daily Users:** 1.2M  
* **MAU:** 9.3M  
* **Daily Visits:** \~32M (GA, 7-day avg)  
* **Daily Ad Requests:** \~900K (GAM, 7-day avg)

### **Geo Distribution (Top 20 Markets)**

| Country | Views % | Active Users % |
| :---- | ----- | ----- |
| Russia | 34.01% | 28.72% |
| Kazakhstan | 6.52% | 9.68% |
| Poland | 6.36% | 4.94% |
| Ukraine | 4.52% | 3.99% |
| Türkiye | 4.26% | 3.09% |
| Germany | 3.01% | 3.33% |
| United States | 2.79% | 3.79% |
| Belarus | 2.27% | 1.72% |
| Romania | 2.21% | 2.22% |
| Czechia | 1.98% | 1.81% |
| Italy | 1.78% | 1.63% |
| France | 1.63% | 1.67% |
| Brazil | 1.48% | 1.54% |
| Spain | 1.44% | 1.35% |
| United Kingdom | 1.41% | 1.49% |
| Netherlands | 1.32% | 1.21% |
| Sweden | 1.31% | 1.09% |
| Serbia | 1.24% | 1.20% |
| Lithuania | 1.19% | 1.16% |
| India | 1.12% | 1.04% |

## 

## 

## 

## 

## 

## 

## 

## **North Star Metrics**

As we won’t be able to split the inventory in an exact 50/50, to determine the winning prebid implementation, we will measure:

### **Primary Metric**

* **~~Revenue Index \=~~** ~~Fill-rate × eCPM across countries in tier~~  
* **Total Revenue per Page View**

After an initial analysis, we observed low eCPMs alongside unusually high fill rates from Publift compared to Mobalytics, which did not align with expected performance outcomes. Following a deeper investigation, we identified that Publift maintains a GAM line item used as a placeholder [for unfilled impressions](https://admanager.google.com/41188963#delivery/line_item/detail/line_item_id=7191036925&li_tab=settings). This artificially inflates the fill-rate reported in the Publift dashboard.

![][image1]

As a result, we decided to shift our evaluation framework towards more reliable metrics based on page views, since monetisation on the platform operates on a one-ad-per-page model. To support this, we implemented custom GA tracking triggered on page load events, segmented by experiment cohort

![][image2] 

### **Secondary Metrics**

* **Net Revenues**  
* **Network activity**

## **Constraints & Experiment Requirements**

To ensure a fair and unbiased test:

* **Publift knowledge:** no experiment awareness   
* **Russian traffic must be excluded** (regulatory and monetisation constraints)  
* **Ad blockers must be accounted for** (affects traffic addressability)  
* Same number of SSPs and comparable configurations across both setups  
  ![][image3]  
* No overlapping demand that could bias one setup over the other

## **Tech Analysis for AB test**

[Experiment: Publift/Prebid A/B Test on CSStats](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1SGhNznCti8O-6lq3fl27zSy6G1D8p0gcnuGxuCciRdo/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.4oyrzwinz6h)
